name: PH-08 Supervisor Watchdog — GPU deployment
executed_by: claude.ai via Desktop Commander on DESKTOP-A2MF76F (GPU, TS 100.95.160.48)

context.goal: Close PH-08 (Supervisor Watchdog persistence). Canonical APEX estate lives on QNAP \\192.168.50.246\Automations\apex\. Laptop (original intended host) is unreliable for always-on operation. GPU is always-on and has network line-of-sight to both QNAP and Internet.
context.previous_state: Watchdog code written in March 2026 used hardcoded C:\Users\bermi\Projects paths (laptop-only). Never registered with Task Scheduler. Supervisor required manual launch after every boot.
context.root_cause_prior_build: wmic deprecated on Windows 11 24H2 builds — the prior watchdog's process-detection helper silently returned 'assume alive' for every check, meaning it would never restart supervisor even when dead.

implementation: Built portable watchdog_gpu.py v1.1 (ref APEX-MB-PY-00011): env-driven APEX_ROOT, psutil-based process scan, Telegram-direct alerts (no ClaudeClaw dependency), state-transition alerting for n8n.
Fixed SyntaxWarning on UNC-path docstring with raw-string prefix.
Created APEX-MB-CFG-00001 run_watchdog.bat wrapper pinning Python 3.11 (which has psutil+requests) and redirecting output to QNAP log.
Registered Windows Task Scheduler job APEX-Watchdog: every 5 minutes under desktop-a2mf76f\owner (no elevation required, inherits user env for TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID).

verification.manual_check_run: python watchdog_gpu.py --check returned supervisor=DOWN, n8n=OK (correct — supervisor was not running pre-bootstrap).
verification.bootstrap_run: Scheduled task fired, watchdog detected DOWN, started supervisor — confirmed running as PID 21440, Python 3.11.
verification.recovery_test: Killed supervisor PID 21440. Triggered APEX-Watchdog manually. New supervisor spawned as PID 39196 within 10s. State file updated.

known_limitations: APEX-Supervisor as a separate scheduled task was NOT created — /sc ONSTART requires elevation. Not needed: the watchdog handles supervisor restart on its first post-boot cycle (max 5-minute delay).
Telegram env vars are User-scope; inherited by the scheduled task running as Owner. If the task is ever migrated to SYSTEM account, the env vars must be promoted to Machine scope.

If the QNAP share is unreachable, the scheduled task will simply fail that cycle — no local fallback. Acceptable: n8n (on QNAP) is down in that scenario anyway, and 5 min later another attempt will be made.
phase_status: PH-08 CERTIFIED — supervisor now auto-recovers from crashes and from cold-start.
